text: HRV-guided training leads to significantly higher maximal velocity in professional endurance runners compared to traditional training over 8 weeks
HRV-guided training results in a higher proportion of moderate intensity training compared to traditional training, which favors more low intensity volume
specific_value: moderate intensity diff: 1.98 ± 0.1%, d = 1.22; low intensity diff: 2.03 ± 0.74%, d = 1.36

text: Traditional training produces significantly higher total training volume than HRV-guided training, but HRV-guided training achieves better performance outcomes with less volume
Daily morning rMSSD measured over 60 seconds (e.g. via HRV4Training) can be used to guide training intensity decisions in professional runners
Higher LnRMSSD values correlate with better VO2max, suggesting HRV can serve as a proxy for cardiovascular fitness in endurance runners
Changes in LnRMSSD correlate with changes in VO2max over an 8-week training period, making HRV useful for tracking cardiovascular adaptation
HRV-guided training produces a small positive effect on vagal modulation (LnRMSSD) compared to traditional training over 8 weeks, though the difference is not statistically significant
